title: Intelligence Efficiency, Ben Geist | Compile 26
summary: Ben Geist argues that paying more for tokens yields diminishing returns and that intelligence efficiency depends on better context and entropy reduction rather than additional compute. He discusses how models function as entropy reduction machines and contrasts token scaling with context-driven approaches.
writeup: Ben Geist makes the case that spending more on tokens delivers diminishing returns, arguing that intelligence efficiency comes from better context and entropy reduction rather than added compute. He frames models as entropy reduction machines and contrasts raw token scaling with context-driven approaches. The talk touches on related concepts including multimodal learning, sparse attention, and multi-agent systems. The central takeaway: improving the context fed to a model matters more for efficiency than increasing token spend.
